test.name: Expand a bare URL into a full EE citation
test.description: User pastes a raw FamilySearch URL with a long query string and asks the skill to turn it into a proper citation. Tests: (1) the skill treats the URL as a starting point only, (2) strips everything after the first '?' from the URL, (3) expands it into all six citation_detail fields, and (4) produces a formatted EE citation string. No scenario source is needed — the URL is provided inline in the user message.

input.user_message: Can you turn this into a proper citation? https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:M5BT-9KF?from=lynx1USV&treeref=LHSV-XVP&query=name%3DThomas%20Flynn%26birthplace%3DPennsylvania

judge_context: Must strip the query string — the citation URL should be 'https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:M5BT-9KF' with nothing after it
A URL alone is never a complete citation — the skill must expand the structure toward all six citation_detail elements, asking the user for what the URL cannot supply

The skill must NOT guess the record type, year, jurisdiction, person, or any locator from the opaque ARK identifier — none of that is derivable from the URL. Asking the user for those details (or asking them to open the link and describe the record) is the correct behavior; inventing values is a failure
where should follow the layered path: FamilySearch.org → underlying microfilm/original repository, once known

Must NOT create a new src_ source entry for this URL — no source entry exists for it, and creating sources is record-extraction's job. Presenting the citation structure to the user (and directing them to record-extraction to persist it) is the correct outcome
If the skill writes nothing to research.json, skipping validate_research_schema is acceptable; if it modifies any existing source, it must validate
